summary:
Infants born from diabetic mothers often have lower birth weights but not necessarily hyperglycemia or high rates of specific cardiac defects; while caution may apply when using certain medications like beta-agonists at delivery time, an outright ban lacks clear consensus based solely on diabetes status alone."
```
To summarize Agent2’s reasoning into two sentences focusing only on clinically significant information related directly to the options presented:

Infants of diabetic mothers typically exhibit reduced size rather than universal neonatal hypoglycemia, though they do not universally develop severe cardiovascular abnormalities either. The routine administration of beta-agonists as a complete contraindication during labor has less supporting evidence outside case-by-case considerations involving other risk factors. ``` Summary: 
Infants of diabetic mothers usually weigh less at birth compared to those whose mothers don’t have diabetes, although most will not experience immediate life-threatening conditions such as severe heart problems right after birth. There doesn't appear to be sufficient reason to generally prohibit the use of beta-agonists unless specifically indicated by additional patient circumstances." ```

This summary captures both critical aspects concisely within the required sentence limit.
